items.name: Laksa Johor
items.description: Pekat fish broth. Spaghetti, not laksa noodles (yes, on purpose). Ulam, sambal belacan. The way they do it down south.

items.name: Lontong Darat — Kuah Sambal
items.description: Compressed rice cakes with rich, spicy sambal gravy. A Johor breakfast classic.

items.name: Lontong Darat — Kuah Kacang
items.description: The same lontong, draped in thick, nutty peanut gravy. Sweeter, creamier, equally authentic.

items.name: Lontong Darat — Kacang Pool
items.description: The famous Johor bean stew over lontong. Some say the best Kacang Pool in KL.

items.name: Nasi Ambeng Ayam Kicap
items.description: Rice, ayam kicap, mee goreng, serunding, sayur — one plate, one tradition. Built to share, easier to finish.

items.name: Kacang Pool
items.description: Johor specialty fava bean stew, slow-cooked with rempah and served with toasted bread.

items.name: Nasi Lemak Sotong
items.description: Fragrant coconut rice with sambal sotong, cucumber, peanuts, and ikan bilis.

items.name: Nasi Lemak Ayam Goreng
items.description: Classic nasi lemak with crispy fried chicken, sambal, and all the traditional fixings.

items.name: Nasi Briani Daging
items.description: Fragrant basmati rice with tender spiced beef, acar, and curry gravy.

items.name: Nasi Briani Ayam Masak Merah
items.description: Briani rice paired with sweet-tangy tomato chicken curry. A celebration on a plate.

items.name: Mee Rebus
items.description: Yellow noodles in thick, sweet-savoury gravy with bean curd, boiled egg, and greens.

items.name: Mee Bandung
items.description: Noodles in rich tomato-chilli gravy with seafood and egg. Bold, red, unmistakably Johor.

items.name: Soto Ayam
items.description: Fragrant turmeric chicken soup with rice cakes, bean sprouts, and sambal kicap.

items.name: Asam Pedas
items.description: Tangy, spicy tamarind stew with fish and vegetables. A Johor-Melaka heritage dish.

items.name: D'Timer Iced Coffee
items.description: Brewed strong, mixed with condensed milk, served cold. Old-school Johor cafe tradition. Pairs with everything.

items.name: Kopi O
items.description: Traditional black coffee brewed the Malaysian way. Strong, aromatic, no frills.

items.name: Kopi Susu
items.description: Malaysian white coffee with evaporated milk. Smooth, creamy, comforting.

items.name: Teh Tarik
items.description: Malaysian pulled tea. Frothy, creamy, and perfectly sweet.

items.name: Teh O Ais Limau
items.description: Iced lime tea. Refreshing, tangy, the perfect palate cleanser.

items.name: Sirap Bandung
items.description: Rose syrup with milk over ice. A Malaysian classic in pink.

items.name: Limau Ais
items.description: Fresh lime juice with soda water. Simple, sharp, refreshing.

items.name: Mango Smoothie
items.description: Blended fresh mango with ice. Thick, tropical, and naturally sweet.

items.name: Oreo Blended
items.description: Cookies and cream blended with ice. A crowd favourite with the kids.

items.name: Pandan Gula Melaka Cake
items.description: Fluffy pandan sponge with palm sugar caramel. Light, fragrant, and not too sweet.

items.name: Brownies
items.description: Dense, fudgy chocolate brownies. Served warm if you ask nicely.

items.name: Roti Bakar
items.description: Thick-cut toast with kaya and butter. The classic Malaysian tea-time companion.
